description_long: This bonsai features a thick, gnarled trunk with multiple sharp, angular branches extending outward, giving it a dramatic and somewhat wild appearance. The trunk is textured and appears aged, with a natural brown coloration and visible bark details. A single, large, oval-shaped green cactus pad with visible spines is attached to one side of the trunk, contrasting with the otherwise barren branches. At the base, a small cluster of mushrooms with rounded caps and short stems adds an organic touch. The overall composition is visually striking, blending elements of arid desert flora with traditional bonsai aesthetics. The object is compact and would serve as a unique decorative piece in an urban environment.
description: A dramatic bonsai with a thick, twisted trunk, barren angular branches, a large green cactus pad, and a cluster of mushrooms at the base. The mix of desert and woodland elements creates a striking, organic display.
description_view_0: Front view showing the thick trunk, angular branches, the cactus pad attached to the side, and the cluster of mushrooms at the base.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the full profile of the trunk, the cactus pad's flat face, and the mushrooms clearly separated from the main trunk.
description_view_2: Rear view focusing on the cactus pad's spiny surface and the back of the trunk, with branches extending outward.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the cactus pad from the front, the trunk's curvature, and the mushrooms positioned to the right.
attribute_pot_type: No visible pot; the base is naturalistic, resembling exposed roots or trunk with mushrooms.
attribute_trunk_curvature: The trunk is sharply twisted and gnarled, with multiple abrupt bends and a dynamic, informal upright style.
attribute_branch_arrangement: Branches are sparse, angular, and irregularly spaced, radiating outward in a dramatic, almost chaotic pattern.
attribute_leaf_density: Extremely sparse, with only a single large cactus pad providing foliage; the rest of the branches are bare.
